diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Human African trypanosomiasis (HAT), caused by Trypanosoma brucei gambiense
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient exhibited symptoms such as psychiatric disorders, hypersomnolence, and persistent fever. A HAT Rapid Diagnostic Test (RDT) was positive, followed by positive immune trypanolysis (TL) test and Card Agglutination Test for Trypanosomiasis (CATT). Parasitological investigations confirmed the presence of trypanosomes in the blood and cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), leading to the diagnosis of stage 2 HAT.
